agents.name: Planner
agents.lastMessage: Introducing aromatic residues at position 5 and hydrophobic residues at position

report.hypothesis: Introducing aromatic residues at position 5 and hydrophobic residues at position 11 will enhance SSTR2-specific binding through improved complementarity with ECL2/TM5, while charge-neutral substitutions at position 6 will reduce off-target SSTR3/SSTR5 interactions, increasing selectivity margin.

agents.name: Critic
agents.lastMessage: The current hypothesis is that disrupting conserved interactions at non-pharmaco

report.hypothesis: The current hypothesis is that disrupting conserved interactions at non-pharmacophore positions will enhance SSTR2 selectivity. The next iteration will test this by introducing focused mutations at positions 1, 2, 5, 6, 11, and 12.

proposed_changes.rationale: To enhance selectivity, we need to disrupt conserved contacts with off-targets SSTR3/SSTR5. Focusing on non-pharmacophore positions 1, 2, 5, 6, 11, and 12 allows us to introduce mutations that reduce off-target binding while preserving the pharmacophore core.
These mutations are designed to break conserved hydrogen bonds and hydrophobic interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5 while maintaining the overall peptide fold and pharmacophore integrity.
